feeSchedule.source: Kalshi published taker fee schedule, CONFIRMED against five authenticated UFC Quick Order tickets (2026-07-16): $100 x3 at prices 0.59/0.69/0.89, plus $50 and $500 at 0.59 which vary size at a fixed price
feeSchedule.note: quadratic in price: maximal near $0.50, minimal at the wings

verifiedScope.establishes: taker rate 0.07 and ceil-to-cent rounding, for single-price YES fills on KXUFCFIGHT as of 2026-07-16. Five authenticated Quick Order tickets reproduce exactly (diff 0.00). The admissible rate interval under ceil is (0.06995195, 0.07000213] — 50ppm wide, containing 0.07 as its only two-decimal value. ceil is discriminated: floor fits 0/5, round-half-up fits 2/5 — it reproduces only the two tickets whose exact cents happen to land above the .5 boundary. LINEARITY IN CONTRACTS IS MEASURED over a 10x span at a fixed price, not assumed: at 0.59, 82.37 -> 164.76 -> 823.81 contracts (10.0013x end to end) produce exact pre-ceil fees 1.394771 -> 2.789881 -> 13.949575 (10.0013x), refuting every rival exponent k*gross^x for x in 0.8-1.5. The ceil premium is observed rather than inferred: it adds 0.375% of the fee at 82.37 contracts and 0.003% at 823.81.
doesNotEstablish: MAKER fees. No maker example exists. `makerRate` has been REMOVED rather than left at an untested 0.0 — see below.
Settlement/exercise fees — a separate Kalshi line item the order ticket never displays.

Linearity in `contracts` at prices OTHER than 0.59. Size was varied at one price only, so the separable form k*C*P*(1-P) is measured on its two axes independently — linear in C at P=0.59, quadratic in P at C~111-165 — and the cross-term is inferred from separability rather than observed. A non-separable rival (an exponent on C that itself varies with P) is not excluded, though nothing suggests one.

The P=0.50 peak — the very figure that motivates this gate. Tested p(1-p) is 0.2419/0.2139/0.0979; the 0.2500 maximum is never observed. The '1.75c at 50c' number is interpolated from the fitted model, not measured.
Prices outside 0.59-0.89. Below 0.59 is covered only by the formula's own p<->1-p symmetry, which cannot validate itself.
The NO side. Symmetry makes NO@0.31 identical to YES@0.69 only if Kalshi bills side-agnostically, which no YES-only example tests.

Sizes below 82.37 contracts, where the ceil premium grows without bound as size shrinks: 1 contract at 0.50 costs $0.02 = 4.0% of notional vs 3.5% asymptotically. The premium is measured at the 82.37 floor (0.375% of the fee) but nothing below it is tested. NOTE: at a $5k bankroll the 0.5% cap proposes ~42 contracts, still below this floor and still correctly caveated; a $10k bankroll proposes ~85, which is inside.
Multi-fill ladders — the actual production path. priceOrder applies ONE ceil to the blended average price; every ticket is a single-price fill. Direction is not uniformly conservative.
Any series other than KXUFCFIGHT (other series carry different published rates), or any date other than 2026-07-16.
verifiedScope.examples: data/fee-examples.json — five unsubmitted Kalshi UFC Quick Order tickets ($50 x1, $100 x3, $500 x1)

feeSchedule.makerNote: maker fees are unverified AND unimplemented; a maker order is refused, not guessed

caps.rationale: initial safety caps, deliberately conservative; never widen them on a winning streak

decisions.contractWording: Will Ezra Elliott win the Elliott vs Anderson professional MMA fight scheduled for Jul 18, 2026?
decisions.settlementRules: If Ezra Elliott wins the Elliott vs Anderson professional MMA fight originally scheduled for Jul 18, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.
decisions.probabilityModelStatus: outright winner — priced from the fighter win probability
decisions.reason: the forecast's prior is an OPENING line (LOGICAL_OPEN) but this contract trades at a CURRENT price. Any difference between them is the market's move since the open, not an edge. A live position requires a wall-clock baseline (tier A/C) contemporaneous with the traded price.
